county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Russellville. One of Kentucky's first counties after statehood (1792 from Lincoln). South-central Kentucky near Tennessee line. Logan County Historical Society. Russellville was temporary Confederate capital of Kentucky (1861). Red River Meeting House — Great Revival of 1800 records. Virginia military warrants south of Green River. FamilySearch microfilm. Major parent county for several others.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1792 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1792. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Logan County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1792.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Eddyville. Formed 1854 from Caldwell. Western Kentucky. Lyon County Historical Society. Eddyville is site of Kentucky State Penitentiary (1884) — inmate records. Kuttawa and Eddyville river town records. Land Between the Lakes displaced families. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Caldwell County for pre-1854 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1854 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1854. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Lyon County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1854.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Richmond. One of Kentucky's original counties from Virginia era (1785). Bluegrass region. Eastern Kentucky University archives and special collections. Madison County Historical Society. Fort Boonesborough connection. Battle of Richmond (1862) Civil War records. Berea College (1855, interracial) archives are exceptional — Appalachian heritage collection. White Hall State Historic Site (Cassius Clay). Extensive enslaved persons records for Bluegrass plantations. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1785 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1785. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1785.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Land Grants — Madison County Area
source_profiles.repository: Library of Virginia, Richmond / KDLA, Frankfort
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-statehood Virginia land grants, surveys, and patents for Kentucky District. Virginia military bounty warrants (south of Green River). Kentucky was part of Virginia until 1 June 1792.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well preserved at LVA and KDLA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Salyersville. Formed 1860 from Floyd, Johnson, and Morgan. Eastern Kentucky mountains. Magoffin County Historical Society. Licking River heritage. Strong Unionist area during Civil War — Magoffin County's secession-sympathizing namesake (Gov. Beriah Magoffin) was ironically unwelcome there. Subsistence farming community. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1860 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1860. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Magoffin County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1860.

source_profiles.source_name: Magoffin County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lebanon. Formed 1834 from Washington. Central Kentucky. Marion County Heritage Center. Lebanon is a regional center with city records. Maker's Mark Distillery records (Loretto area). Sisters of Loretto convent records (Catholic heritage). Holy Cross community records. Strong Catholic population — church records essential. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1834 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1834. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Marion County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1834.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Benton. Formed 1842 from Calloway. Western Kentucky (Jackson Purchase region). Marshall County Historical Society. Kentucky Dam and Kentucky Lake (TVA, 1944) — many families displaced. Benton city records. Jonathan Creek community. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Calloway County for pre-1842 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1842 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1842. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Marshall County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1842.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1870 from Floyd, Johnson, Lawrence, Pike.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Inez. Formed 1870 from Floyd, Johnson, Lawrence, and Pike. Eastern Kentucky mountains on the Tug Fork of the Big Sandy. Martin County Historical Society. Hatfield-McCoy feud territory — some court records at KDLA. Coal mining records. Inez is one of Kentucky's most isolated county seats. FamilySearch microfilm. Check four parent counties for pre-1870 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1870 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1870. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Martin County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1870.

source_profiles.source_name: Martin County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Maysville. Formed 1788 from Bourbon (Virginia era). One of Kentucky's earliest counties. Maysville is a historic Ohio River port — gateway for settlers entering Kentucky from Pennsylvania and Virginia. Mason County Museum. Underground Railroad connections (Harriet Beecher Stowe's inspiration for Uncle Tom's Cabin). Maysville city directories from 1850s. Kentucky Gateway Museum Center. Extensive early records from 1789. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1788 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1788. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Mason County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1788.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Paducah. Formed 1824 from Hickman. Western Kentucky on the Ohio River. McCracken County Historical Society. Paducah is a major river city — extensive river commerce records. Paducah Public Library local history. William Clark Market House Museum. Paducah city directories from 1870s. Railroad records (Illinois Central). Strong African American community — Lincoln School records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1824 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1824. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1824.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Whitley City. Formed 1912 from Pulaski, Wayne, and Whitley — Kentucky's second-youngest county. Southeastern Kentucky mountains. Big South Fork National River and Recreation Area — NPS records document displaced families. McCreary County Historical Society. Stearns Coal and Lumber Company records document company town families (Stearns, Blue Heron). FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1912 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1912. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
